items.prompt: Which statement best describes data collection in this chapter?
choices: Any export from a production database
The systematic process of gathering and measuring information to answer questions with evidence
The step of training machine learning models on labeled rows
items.explain: The chapter defines collection as a systematic, evidence-oriented process that precedes curation and analysis.

items.prompt: In the five-step collection plan, what typically comes immediately after defining objectives?
choices: Identifying sources (primary vs secondary, and so on)
items.explain: The plan order is objectives, sources, methods, execution with quality checks, then storage and management.

items.prompt: Effective collection programs should be systematic, objective-oriented, and dynamic.
items.explain: These three process traits balance rigor with adaptability as new instruments and streams appear.

items.prompt: In Example 2.1, name two elements of the retail cart-abandonment collection plan besides the objective.
items.rubric: Any two of: sources, methods, sample, storage/schema.
items.sample_answer: Sources (survey plus web analytics), methods (questionnaire and server logs), sample (abandoners stratified by device), and anonymized CSV storage with documented schema.
